plan_units.learning_objective: Recognize that core physician traits directly map to innovation leadership traits, positioning clinicians as natural innovators.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From seeing innovation as someone else’s role to recognizing physicianship itself as a foundation for innovation leadership through trait-mapping.
plan_units.rationale: This opener establishes the module’s core mindset shift: physicians do not need a new identity to innovate; they can transfer existing clinical capabilities to system problems.
source_refs: You do not need to learn
plan_units.title: Physicianship = Innovation Leadership

plan_units.learning_objective: Explain how the clinical method maps directly onto design thinking steps for healthcare innovation.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From viewing design thinking as business jargon to understanding it as an extension of everyday clinical reasoning through direct workflow mapping.
plan_units.rationale: This concept is central, sequential, and pedagogically heavy. It benefits from progressive disclosure because learners must move from a familiar workflow to an innovation framework without overload.
plan_units.title: The Bridge: Diagnosis = Design Thinking

plan_units.learning_objective: Identify the specific correspondences between history-taking, diagnosis, treatment planning, and design thinking stages.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From viewing design thinking as business jargon to understanding it as an extension of everyday clinical reasoning through direct workflow mapping.
plan_units.rationale: This interstitial deepens the head concept by isolating the pairwise mappings so learners can process each correspondence clearly.
source_refs: When you take a patient's history
the first step of Design Thinking
synthesize the physical exam and labs
plan_units.title: Clinical Workflow to Innovation Workflow Mapping

plan_units.learning_objective: Apply the design thinking analogy by reframing the innovation target from individual patient care to broken workflows and systems.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From viewing design thinking as business jargon to understanding it as an extension of everyday clinical reasoning through direct workflow mapping.
plan_units.rationale: This interstitial resolves the cluster by landing the transfer: the clinician already runs the cycle, but now applies it to operational systems.
plan_units.title: Changing the Patient: From Person to Workflow

plan_units.learning_objective: Describe intrapreneurship as a structured, replicable approach to driving change from within an organization.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From treating intrapreneurship as a vague personality trait to understanding it as a structured internal change formula through guided reading and framing.
plan_units.rationale: The source explicitly requires this reading block before the intrapreneurship comparison and frames it as foundational for later stakeholder alignment work.
plan_units.title: Required Reading: The Intrapreneurship Formula

plan_units.learning_objective: Differentiate entrepreneurship from intrapreneurship and identify why internal innovation is the clinician’s most immediate arena for impact.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From conflating innovation with leaving the system to distinguishing intrapreneurship as the path to internal healthcare change through contrastive definition.
plan_units.rationale: This is a high-value comparison slide with gestalt value as a simultaneous split-screen contrast; it should remain dense rather than be decomposed.
plan_units.title: Intrapreneurship vs. Entrepreneurship

plan_units.learning_objective: Understand why intrapreneurship is harder than entrepreneurship because innovation must occur within legacy institutional constraints.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From seeing internal innovation as merely a location difference to appreciating its structural difficulty through the load-bearing blueprint metaphor.
plan_units.rationale: This visual metaphor introduces tension in the learner’s understanding by making internal constraints vivid and memorable.
plan_units.title: The Intrapreneur's Maze: Innovating Inside Load-Bearing Structures

plan_units.learning_objective: Explain that successful intrapreneurship requires respecting legacy IT, compliance, politics, and budgets while redesigning workflows.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From seeing internal innovation as merely a location difference to appreciating its structural difficulty through the load-bearing blueprint metaphor.
plan_units.rationale: This resolve interstitial converts the metaphor into the practical takeaway for the course’s purpose.
source_refs: redesign the room without breaking the building
plan_units.title: Redesign the Room Without Breaking the Building

plan_units.learning_objective: Distinguish a raw idea from an actionable opportunity vetted for feasibility, viability, and desirability within a specific organization.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From equating creativity with value to recognizing that only vetted opportunities are actionable through disciplined feasibility, viability, and desirability testing.
plan_units.rationale: This is one of the module’s most important threshold concepts. Misunderstanding it undermines the rest of the lesson, so clustering is warranted.
source_refs: An idea is simply a thought
rigorously vetted for feasibility, viability, and desirability
plan_units.title: Idea vs. Opportunity

plan_units.learning_objective: Recognize that solution-first thinking does not create value until linked to an executable organizational opportunity.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From equating creativity with value to recognizing that only vetted opportunities are actionable through disciplined feasibility, viability, and desirability testing.
plan_units.rationale: This tension interstitial surfaces the misconception that idea generation alone is sufficient.
source_refs: We should use AI to write notes
On its own, its value is zero
plan_units.title: Why Ideas Alone Have Zero Value

plan_units.learning_objective: Explain the criteria that transform an idea into an opportunity: feasibility, viability, desirability, and execution path.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From equating creativity with value to recognizing that only vetted opportunities are actionable through disciplined feasibility, viability, and desirability testing.
plan_units.rationale: This develop interstitial deepens the operational test learners must apply to their own proposals.
plan_units.title: Engineering the Opportunity

plan_units.learning_objective: Apply rigorous problem framing by contrasting a superficial solution idea with a root-cause-based opportunity statement.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From equating creativity with value to recognizing that only vetted opportunities are actionable through disciplined feasibility, viability, and desirability testing.
plan_units.rationale: This resolve interstitial exemplifies the threshold concept in concrete operational language learners can emulate.
source_refs: buy iPads for all the nurses
plan_units.title: Worked Example: From Superficial Solution to Opportunity Framing

plan_units.learning_objective: Explain first principles thinking as the method for breaking broken workflows into fundamental truths and rebuilding better systems.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From patching the status quo by analogy to rebuilding systems from fundamental truths through first-principles deconstruction.
plan_units.rationale: This concept extends opportunity framing into redesign method. It is important but communicates well as a visual progression without extra fragmentation.
source_refs: Breaking it down to fundamental truths
Building a new system from the ground up
plan_units.title: The Deconstruction: First Principles Thinking

plan_units.learning_objective: Summarize the module’s three core takeaways: clinicians are already equipped, intrapreneurship is the arena, and opportunities matter more than ideas.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From a set of separate concepts to an integrated innovation stance through concise recap and forward transition.
plan_units.rationale: This is a synthesis slide whose value lies in presenting the simultaneous whole and preparing the learner for the next module.
plan_units.title: Part 3 Summary and Transition

plan_units.learning_objective: Demonstrate recall and understanding of key distinctions: idea vs. opportunity, history vs. empathize, intrapreneurship formula, problem-love over solution-love, and lead domino root-cause framing.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From exposure to verification through targeted recall and understanding checks aligned to the module’s threshold concepts.
plan_units.rationale: The source includes a complete formative assessment set, so the lesson plan should preserve it as the module’s verification layer.
source_refs: fall in love with the problem
plan_units.title: Part 3 Knowledge Check

research_goals.pedagogical_intent: Learner needs the primary-source basis for Sandra Lam’s Chapter 4 intrapreneurship framing so the workbook can quote and contextualize the formula accurately.
Learner needs the authoritative source for the HII slide 23 image so the workbook can preserve fidelity while explaining the idea-versus-opportunity distinction.
Learner needs a faithful rendering basis for the intrapreneurship formula venn diagram reference on page 48 so the workbook can support close reading of the model.

depth_delta.deferred_depth: A fuller side-by-side explanation of each mapped step, including how history-taking aligns with empathize, diagnostic synthesis aligns with define, and treatment planning aligns with ideate/prototype in operational settings.
depth_delta.retained_on_slide: The glanceable paired workflow map and headline analogy.

exercises.prompt_intent: Have learners restate each clinical-to-design-thinking mapping in their own words.
Have learners transfer the mapping to a non-patient operational workflow they know.

sections.narrative_intent: Give learners the detailed connective tissue that the slide should not carry, so the deck can remain visual and fast.
sections.title: Clinical Method to Design Thinking Mapping Guide

depth_delta.deferred_depth: A guided reading frame emphasizing intrapreneurship as a structured formula, with prompts on organizational realities, internal resources, and stakeholder alignment.
depth_delta.retained_on_slide: The reading requirement and its framing purpose.

exercises.prompt_intent: Prompt learners to summarize how the reading turns a vague concept into a replicable approach.

sections.narrative_intent: Support close reading and reflection without bloating slide narration.
sections.title: Reading Companion: The Intrapreneurship Formula

depth_delta.deferred_depth: A workbook matrix for identifying local examples of legacy IT, compliance, hospital politics, and operating budgets in the learner’s own setting.
depth_delta.retained_on_slide: The metaphor of building inside a load-bearing blueprint.

exercises.prompt_intent: Ask learners to sort observed barriers into structural categories rather than treating them as generic resistance.
Have learners identify which constraint is load-bearing versus merely inconvenient in a chosen workflow.

sections.narrative_intent: Translate the metaphor into institution-specific analysis.
sections.title: Constraint Map for the Intrapreneur's Maze

depth_delta.deferred_depth: A stepwise worksheet for converting a solution idea into a problem statement, root-cause framing, and opportunity definition tested for feasibility, viability, desirability, and execution path.
depth_delta.retained_on_slide: The threshold distinction between idea and opportunity.

exercises.answer_key_source_ref: Chief Complaint: Discharge times are delayed
exercises.prompt_intent: Have learners rewrite a solution-first idea as a chief complaint and root-cause statement.
Ask learners to diagnose why a proposed idea fails opportunity criteria.
Have learners draft an original opportunity statement for their own unit.

sections.narrative_intent: Carry the procedural rigor and productive struggle that would overcrowd the slide deck.
sections.title: Idea-to-Opportunity Worksheet

depth_delta.deferred_depth: A structured page for listing the current workflow, decomposing it into fundamental truths, and sketching a rebuilt process from the same raw elements.
depth_delta.retained_on_slide: The three-part visual of old system, raw blocks, and rebuilt system.

exercises.prompt_intent: Guide learners to separate inherited assumptions from actual workflow necessities.

exercises.answer_key_source_ref: Building a new system from the ground up
exercises.prompt_intent: Ask learners to redesign a workflow from the deconstructed components.

sections.narrative_intent: Enable hands-on system redesign thinking beyond the slide’s visual metaphor.
sections.title: First Principles Deconstruction Template

depth_delta.deferred_depth: A reflective answer-and-rationale companion that explains why each correct answer is right and why distractors fail, with space for self-correction.

exercises.prompt_intent: Reinforce key vocabulary distinctions from the assessment set.

exercises.answer_key_source_ref: fall in love with the problem
exercises.prompt_intent: Have learners explain why the false or incorrect options conflict with the course framework.

sections.narrative_intent: Turn the formative check into a deeper retrieval and misconception-correction tool.
sections.title: Knowledge Check Review and Reflection

lesson_plan.lesson_summary: This lesson reframes clinicians as natural innovators, bridges the clinical method to design thinking, defines intrapreneurship as the primary arena for healthcare change, and moves learners from solution-first ideation toward rigorously framed organizational opportunities. Clustered treatment is applied to the two highest-burden threshold concepts: clinical method to design thinking, and idea versus opportunity. The lesson closes with synthesis and a knowledge check to verify the core distinctions before transition to the next module.

plan_units.rationale: This is one of the module’s most important threshold concepts. Misunderstanding it undermines the rest of the lesson, so clustering is warranted.
source_refs: An idea is simply a thought
rigorously vetted for feasibility, viability, and desirability
plan_units.title: Idea vs. Opportunity

plan_units.learning_objective: Recognize that solution-first thinking does not create value until linked to an executable organizational opportunity.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From equating creativity with value to recognizing that only vetted opportunities are actionable through disciplined feasibility, viability, and desirability testing.
plan_units.rationale: This tension interstitial surfaces the misconception that idea generation alone is sufficient.
source_refs: We should use AI to write notes
On its own, its value is zero
plan_units.title: Why Ideas Alone Have Zero Value

plan_units.learning_objective: Explain the criteria that transform an idea into an opportunity: feasibility, viability, desirability, and execution path.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From equating creativity with value to recognizing that only vetted opportunities are actionable through disciplined feasibility, viability, and desirability testing.
plan_units.rationale: This develop interstitial deepens the operational test learners must apply to their own proposals.
plan_units.title: Engineering the Opportunity

plan_units.learning_objective: Apply rigorous problem framing by contrasting a superficial solution idea with a root-cause-based opportunity statement.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From equating creativity with value to recognizing that only vetted opportunities are actionable through disciplined feasibility, viability, and desirability testing.
plan_units.rationale: This resolve interstitial exemplifies the threshold concept in concrete operational language learners can emulate.
source_refs: buy iPads for all the nurses
plan_units.title: Worked Example: From Superficial Solution to Opportunity Framing

plan_units.learning_objective: Explain first principles thinking as the method for breaking broken workflows into fundamental truths and rebuilding better systems.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From patching the status quo by analogy to rebuilding systems from fundamental truths through first-principles deconstruction.
plan_units.rationale: This concept extends opportunity framing into redesign method. It is important but communicates well as a visual progression without extra fragmentation.
source_refs: Breaking it down to fundamental truths
Building a new system from the ground up
plan_units.title: The Deconstruction: First Principles Thinking

plan_units.learning_objective: Summarize the module’s three core takeaways: clinicians are already equipped, intrapreneurship is the arena, and opportunities matter more than ideas.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From a set of separate concepts to an integrated innovation stance through concise recap and forward transition.
plan_units.rationale: This is a synthesis slide whose value lies in presenting the simultaneous whole and preparing the learner for the next module.
plan_units.title: Part 3 Summary and Transition

plan_units.learning_objective: Demonstrate recall and understanding of key distinctions: idea vs. opportunity, history vs. empathize, intrapreneurship formula, problem-love over solution-love, and lead domino root-cause framing.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From exposure to verification through targeted recall and understanding checks aligned to the module’s threshold concepts.
plan_units.rationale: The source includes a complete formative assessment set, so the lesson plan should preserve it as the module’s verification layer.

plan_units.title: Part 3 Knowledge Check
